parsing_approach: Real XML parse via xml.sax with a locator (external entity/DTD resolution disabled), modeled on the maven-pom gallery tool. A cheap root-element sniff dispatches each file to the right handler: <packages> is packages.config (classic non-SDK-style projects, one Dependency node per <package>); <Project> is a .csproj, which yields a BuildModule node (name from the file stem, target framework(s) from <TargetFramework>/<TargetFrameworkVersion>/<TargetFrameworks>) plus a Dependency node per <PackageReference> and a ProjectReference node+edge per <ProjectReference>. Files with neither root element yield an empty fragment rather than guessing.
extracts: Dependency node per <package id version targetFramework> in packages.config, carrying the exact declaration line
Dependency node per <PackageReference Include Version> in SDK-style .csproj (both attribute-style; see known_limitations for the child-element form)
BuildModule node per .csproj with target_frameworks from the property group
ProjectReference node + references edge per <ProjectReference Include> (inter-module dependency, e.g. Curriculums.csproj -> Domain.Models.csproj)
known_limitations: Classic non-SDK-style .csproj (xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003", the common case in this repository) declares assembly references via <Reference Include="..."> with an optional <HintPath>, not <PackageReference> — those are NOT extracted as Dependency nodes by this tool. Only packages.config in the same project captures the NuGet-sourced subset of those references. A legacy .csproj with zero <PackageReference> elements will correctly yield a BuildModule with no dependencies, which is expected, not a parsing failure.

PackageReference version supplied as a nested <Version> child element (rather than a Version= attribute) is not extracted — only the attribute form, which is what modern SDK-style csproj files normally use.
No resolution against the NuGet lock file or a package's actual dependency tree (transitive dependencies) — only what each file directly declares.

ProjectReference target paths are recorded as raw (often Windows-style, backslash-separated) strings; resolving them to an actual BuildModule/File node in this repository is the semantic-graph builder's job.
